notes: URL params (manuscriptId, evaluationJobId) override dashboard default. Dashboard default = latest completed evaluation.
Model is configurable via AGENT_READINESS_MODEL env var (default gpt-4o). maxDuration=60s. TIMEOUT_MS=45_000.
Word minimums: query_letter ≥200w, synopsis ≥150w, author_bio ≥50w, what_makes_unique ≥60w. query_pitch and comparables have no minimum.
Upsert (user_id, manuscript_id, section_type) — regeneration overwrites existing draft.

KNOWN GAP: The Approve button in AgentReadinessClient.tsx is a disabled stub with no onClick handler. Approval is tracked only in React state (sectionStates). No API endpoint writes status=approved to DB. The getPackageStatuses() DB check therefore always returns Not Started or Draft, never Approved. This is the highest-priority seam for the next AR sprint.

supplier: System (derived from agent_readiness_sections in DB, or client sectionStates)
notes: KNOWN GAP: Completeness check is designed around approved status but approval is not persisted. The actual CTA gate is allSectionsStarted (every section has a draft). Full approval enforcement requires: (1) section approval API, (2) client calling it on Approve click, (3) DB read on page load.
This is a convenience orchestration of AR02×5, not a distinct assembly pipeline stage. It returns draft content only. Package assembly and section approval are separate downstream author steps.
Additional export targets (Copy Query Package, Save Package Version, Submit to Storygate Studio) are in doctrine scope but not yet fully implemented.
Package persistence is a known future seam. Until package records exist, history page is a placeholder.
